Entity/Subject: Operation Epic Fury — US & Israel Strike Iran
Key Player/Organization: U.S., Israel, IDF, Iran, IRGC
Expected Impact/Value: US and Israel launch Operation Epic Fury on Feb 28, targeting Iran's nuclear and military infrastructure. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed. Six US service members killed. Trump signals an extended campaign lasting weeks.

Entity/Subject: What Comes Next After Operation Epic Fury?
Key Player/Organization: Atlantic Council, US, Israel, Iran
Expected Impact/Value: Multi-expert analysis: the assault has decapitated Iran's regime but risks cascading instability across the Middle East. Key questions include: succession in Tehran, proxy network coherence, and US exit strategy.

Entity/Subject: China Playing the Long Game on Iran
Key Player/Organization: China, Russia, Iran, UN Security Council
Expected Impact/Value: Russia and China condemn strikes but offer Iran no material support, exposing the limits of their 'axis.' Beijing positions for post-war reconstruction influence while Moscow's regional leverage is further eroded.

Entity/Subject: Iran's Proxies Acting Independently — Hezbollah, Houthis, Iraq Militias
Key Player/Organization: Hezbollah, Houthis, Iran-backed Iraqi militias
Expected Impact/Value: Hezbollah fires rockets into Israel; Iraq militias target US troops; Houthis strike Israeli targets. Foreign Policy reports proxies are each calculating their own survival — not acting as a unified 'axis of resistance.'

Entity/Subject: Strait of Hormuz — Global Economic Chokepoint
Key Player/Organization: Iran, OPEC, Gulf States, Global Shipping
Expected Impact/Value: Iran threatens Strait of Hormuz closure — 20% of global seaborne crude and LNG at risk. Insurance withdrawal has caused de facto closure. Analysts warn of $100+ oil and a 'guaranteed global recession' if disruption is sustained.

Entity/Subject: U.S.-Iran Escalation
Key Player/Organization: EU, IRGC, Iran
Expected Impact/Value: EU officially designates Iran's IRGC as a terrorist organization; Iran retaliates by labeling all EU militaries as terrorists.

Entity/Subject: Munich Security Conference 2026
Key Player/Organization: Zelenskyy, Rubio, Merz, Starmer
Expected Impact/Value: Global leaders debate 'Under Destruction' theme; German Chancellor Merz calls for integrated European NATO.

Entity/Subject: New START Expiration
Key Player/Organization: U.S., Russia, UN
Expected Impact/Value: Last nuclear arms treaty expires; UN Secretary-General Guterres warns of 'grave moment' for global security.

Entity/Subject: Sudan Atrocities Inquiry
Key Player/Organization: UN News, Volker Türk
Expected Impact/Value: UN human rights chief warns of 'apocalyptic' atrocities in Darfur; calls for urgent international intervention.

Entity/Subject: HRW World Report 2026
Key Player/Organization: Human Rights Watch
Expected Impact/Value: Annual report highlights 'global system in peril'; details 72% of world living under autocracy.

Entity/Subject: Global Economy Growth Surge
Key Player/Organization: IMF, China, India, Indonesia
Expected Impact/Value: China (26.6%) and India (17%) drive global GDP growth in 2026; global economy races ahead despite geopolitical turbulence.

Entity/Subject: U.S. Clean Air Act Rollbacks
Key Player/Organization: EPA, EDF, Coal Industry
Expected Impact/Value: Trump administration grants sweeping Clean Air Act exemptions to coal plants; environmental groups challenge legality in court.

Entity/Subject: Extreme Heat Crisis — South Asia
Key Player/Organization: WHO, WMO, India, Pakistan, Bangladesh
Expected Impact/Value: New WMO-WHO initiatives launched to address extreme heat mortality in South Asia; region faces 50°C+ forecasts by summer 2026.

Entity/Subject: EU AI Act Full Enforcement
Key Player/Organization: European Commission, Meta, OpenAI, Google
Expected Impact/Value: EU AI Act enters full enforcement; high-risk AI systems now require conformity assessments. Tech giants scramble to comply or face €35M fines.

Entity/Subject: Deep-Sea Mining Moratorium Debate
Key Player/Organization: ISA, Pacific Island Nations, China, Norway
Expected Impact/Value: International Seabed Authority faces showdown: Pacific Island nations demand moratorium while China and Norway push for commercial extraction licenses.

Entity/Subject: Horn of Africa Mega-War Risk — Ethiopia, Eritrea & Sudan Conflict Merger
Key Player/Organization: Ethiopia (Abiy Ahmed), Eritrea (Isaias Afwerki), Sudan SAF, RSF, TPLF, UAE
Expected Impact/Value: Ethiopia on verge of resuming war against Tigray and threatening Eritrean invasion for Red Sea port access. Sudan's SAF formalizing military ties with Eritrea and TPLF factions. Ethiopia covertly training RSF fighters. ICG warns of 'regional mega-war' — a one-conflict ecosystem merging the Horn's deadliest wars. 15M already displaced in Sudan. UAE and rival Arab states risk being pulled into proxy escalation.

Entity/Subject: Sudan Civil War — World's Largest Humanitarian Crisis
Key Player/Organization: Sudan SAF, RSF, UN OCHA, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt
Expected Impact/Value: Sudan remains the world's largest humanitarian crisis: 15M displaced over 3 years, 21M facing crisis food insecurity, some facing famine. RSF accused of genocide and ethnic cleansing in Darfur. UAE arms shipments via Somalia documented enabling el-Fasher massacre. Eastern Sudan destabilization risk as conflict could merge with Ethiopian tensions.

Entity/Subject: Myanmar Humanitarian Catastrophe — 16M Need Aid
Key Player/Organization: Myanmar Tatmadaw (SAC), Arakan Army, KNLA, UN OCHA, WFP
Expected Impact/Value: Myanmar's crisis deepens: 3.6M internally displaced (up from 300K pre-2021), projected to reach 4M in 2026. UN projects 16M people need humanitarian aid. Military conducted 2,165 airstrikes in 2025; destroyed Mrauk-U hospital killing 33. March 2025 earthquake killed 3,745; 132 health facilities damaged. GDP contracting 2%, inflation above 20%, poverty above 80%. Rohingya blockade since 2023 leaves 600K IDPs without aid. $890M HNRP appeal but only 2.6M can be reached.

Entity/Subject: China's Grey Zone Strategy — Escalation Without War in the Indo-Pacific
Key Player/Organization: China PLA Navy, Taiwan, Philippines, Japan, AUKUS, Quad
Expected Impact/Value: Justice Mission 2025 was the largest-ever Chinese military exercise around Taiwan — 130+ aircraft, destroyers, and rocket units practicing rapid encirclement. PLA Navy recorded 163 operations in the South China Sea in 2025, with coast guard presence at Scarborough Shoal more than doubled. For the first time, China publicly stated its exercise goal was 'deterrence outside the island chain.' Chinese naval task groups conducted live-fire drills in the Tasman Sea near Australia/NZ. Analysis frames this as a calculated grey-zone strategy to alter regional norms incrementally below the threshold of war.

Entity/Subject: Russia's Spring Offensive — Record Drone Barrage on Ukraine
Key Player/Organization: Russia, Ukraine, ISW, NATO
Expected Impact/Value: Russia fires nearly 1,000 drones and missiles at Ukraine within 24 hours on Mar 23-24 — one of its biggest-ever attacks. 400+ long-range Shahed drones hit seven cities including Kyiv, Lviv, and Dnipro. Lviv's 17th-century UNESCO-heritage St. Andrew's Church set ablaze. ISW confirms Russia's spring-summer offensive is now underway with troop buildup and heavy equipment moved to the front. Iran war has diverted international attention from Ukraine's plight. Ukraine offering drone defense tech to US/Gulf partners in exchange for Patriot missiles.

Entity/Subject: SCOTUS Strikes Down IEEPA Tariffs — Constitutional Trade Crisis
Key Player/Organization: US Supreme Court, Chief Justice Roberts, Trump Administration, Congress
Expected Impact/Value: In Learning Resources v. Trump (Feb 20, 2026), SCOTUS rules 6-3 that IEEPA does not authorize the President to impose tariffs. Roberts writes: 'IEEPA contains no reference to tariffs or duties... no President has read IEEPA to confer such power.' Wharton projects up to $175B in potential refunds to importers. President terminates IEEPA tariffs the same day but pivots to alternative legal authorities. Kavanaugh's 63-page dissent warns of 'billions in refunds' fallout. Landmark ruling reshapes the constitutional balance of trade power between Congress and the Executive.

Entity/Subject: Global Trade Geometry Shift 2026 — AI Boom, US-China Decoupling & EU Squeeze
Key Player/Organization: McKinsey MGI, WTO, ASEAN, US, China, EU
Expected Impact/Value: McKinsey MGI's comprehensive 2026 trade report documents: US-China trade fell ~30% ($130B decline); AI-related hardware trade grew ~40% accounting for 1/3 of total trade growth; US AI imports surged $220B; China shifted from 'factory of the world' to 'factory to the factories' exporting intermediate goods; ASEAN exports jumped 14% as the region becomes the key US-China connector; EU faces a 'double squeeze' — $22B auto trade balance deterioration as Germany imports more Chinese cars than it exports for the first time. Trade grew faster than the global economy despite highest US tariffs since WWII.

Entity/Subject: Brazil-China Soybean Axis Under Stress — Phytosanitary Disruption
Key Player/Organization: Brazil, China, Cargill, StoneX, US soybean exporters
Expected Impact/Value: China tightens phytosanitary checks on Brazilian soybeans, finding live insects, pesticide-coated beans, and heat damage. Cargill pauses Brazil-to-China soybean exports. Brazil supplies 75% of China's soybean imports — disruption threatens world's largest agricultural trade corridor. Freight rates Santos-to-China up 24% amid Iran war. Soybean premium jumps to $1.22/bu over CBOT (from $1.12). China-Brazil subsequently ease rules (Mar 23) but the structural dependency and vulnerability are exposed. US suppliers may gain a window.

Entity/Subject: ASEAN Manufacturing Boom — Strongest PMI in a Decade
Key Player/Organization: ASEAN, Vietnam, Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, Indonesia
Expected Impact/Value: ASEAN manufacturing PMI hits 53.8 in February 2026 — strongest pace in over a decade. Vietnam's $198B industrial sector undergoing structural transformation as global supply chains rewire. Indonesia manufacturing investment soared 150% to $44B. ASEAN exported 14% more in 2025, importing $100B+ more from China while exporting $80B more to the US — confirming the region's role as the critical US-China supply chain intermediary. Semiconductor surge and AI infrastructure build reshaping regional trade architecture.

Entity/Subject: EU Double Squeeze — Auto Sector Crisis as Chinese Competition Surges
Key Player/Organization: EU, Germany, China, BYD, Volkswagen, BMW
Expected Impact/Value: EU faces unprecedented 'double squeeze': rising Chinese imports ($60B+ increase in 2025) and US tariffs constraining exports. Auto sector hardest hit — EU auto trade balance with US and China down $22B. Germany imports more cars from China than it exports for the first time in history. Chinese EV sales in EU surged 50% to 800K+ units. EU captured less than 3% of US demand gap left by China. Manufacturing trade surplus shrank $70B excluding frontloading. EU eases ICE vehicle phase-out timelines and courts $10B/yr in Chinese battery factory investment.

Entity/Subject: AI Hardware Trade Explosion — Semiconductors Reshape Global Commerce
Key Player/Organization: TSMC, NVIDIA, Samsung, ASML, US, Taiwan, South Korea
Expected Impact/Value: AI-related hardware trade grew ~40% in 2025 — accounting for one-third of all global trade growth. US AI imports surged $220B (66% increase), driven by data center buildout consuming chips, servers, and networking equipment from Taiwan, South Korea, and ASEAN. China's AI trade grew only 16% ($85B) due to US export restrictions on advanced chips. US added roughly half the world's new data center capacity. Netherlands/Japan coordinated chip export restrictions. China tightened critical minerals controls. Trade restrictions shape flows but AI infrastructure buildout will continue globally.

Entity/Subject: South China Sea — Scarborough Shoal Confrontation Escalates
Key Player/Organization: China Coast Guard, Philippines, ASEAN, US Indo-Pacific Command
Expected Impact/Value: China Coast Guard more than doubled its presence around Scarborough Shoal compared to 2024, shifting its most aggressive actions from Second Thomas Shoal. PLA Navy recorded 163 operations in the South China Sea in 2025 — a record including live-fire exercises. Philippines faces intensifying maritime pressure as China consolidates grey zone control. ASEAN maritime security framework under strain as individual nations calculate bilateral vs. collective responses.
